title: Efficacy of retreatment in single-rooted mandibular premolars obturated with a novel Bioceramic sealer with two different filling techniques (A comparative in-vitro study).
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ilzaj","text":"Aim of the study","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yu7sk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3r0lw","text":"This study aims to evaluate the remaining filling material, remaining dentin thickness and time required after retreatment of single-rooted mandibular premolars obturated with Neosealer flo and Ceraseal sealers in either lateral or warm vertical compaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0wxtu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"gbs2p","text":"Statement of the problem","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vqt34","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aww7u","text":"The main goal of root canal treatment is to provide three-dimensional obturation of the root canal system. A root canal filling aims to provide a hermetic seal reducing coronal leakage and bacterial contamination, hence entombing the remaining irritants within the root canal and preventing apical periodontitis using gutta-percha in combination with root canal sealers (Kim et al.2019, Hachem et al. 2019).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m3hp0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ughzg","text":"Nowadays, Calcium silicate-based materials have attracted considerable attention because of their outstanding biocompatibility and bioactivity. They enhance the formation of crystalline structures similar to tooth and bone apatite materials, thereby improving sealer-to-root dentin bonding (Al-Haddad and Aziz. 2016). Bioceramic sealers provide a unique model of chemical bonding between the non-soluble calcium silicate materials and the dentin micro-mechanical anchorage caused by the cement plugs within the dentinal tubules (Raghavendra et al. 2017).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mrcwk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9wvr0","text":"Recently, new Bioceramic sealers have been introduced to be used in conjunction with Warm vertical obturation technique. This technique was proven to allow three-dimensional filling of complex root canal systems with canal irregularities and potentially lateral canals. Therefore, the application of warm vertical compaction with bioceramic sealers would improve the filling quality and reduce the likelihood of leakage (Iandolo et al. 2020)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsnsv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62uql","text":"However, retreatment of root canals sealed with bioceramic sealers can present some challenges, among which is incomplete removal of the bioceramic sealer due to their excellent sealing ability and enhanced bonding to dentin walls rendering their complete removal almost impossible (Aref et al. 2023). In addition, removing both the bioceramic sealer and the gutta-percha would be considered a time-consuming procedure (Romeiro et al. 2020). Moreover, bioceramic sealers’ outstanding sealability is facilitated by biomineralization activity that would enhance their adhesion and resistance to dislocation from dentin (McMichael et al. 2015). The hardness of the sealer upon setting would render it difficult to be removed completely during retreatment procedure.(Zuolo et al. 2015)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ezocf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rwyd8","text":"Reciprocating instruments have been previously tested for complete removal of the filling material. The Reciproc (VDW, Munich, Germany), which was designed for complete instrumentation of the root canal with a single instrument has shown promising results in terms of effectiveness in removing filling material, safety and decrease time in lab research (Zuolo et al. 2013).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"onvlr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ztitd","text":"Thus the aim of this study is to evaluate the remaining filling material, remaining dentin thickness and time required for removal of bioceramic obturating material during the retreatment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jelve","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ynt8v","text":"procedure in single-rooted mandibular premolars using Neosealer flo and Ceraseal sealers in either lateral or warm vertical compaction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"seldi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The external root surfaces of the teeth will be cleaned with a curette to remove calculus and hard deposits and then will be soaked in 5.25% Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) for 30 minutes to remove soft tissue debris. The teeth will then be stored in sterile saline till use.
All teeth will be examined under dental operating microscope at 10X to ensure absence of caries, cracks or fractures.
Pre-operative radiographs will be taken from buccolingual and mesiodistal aspects to assess the presence of a single, mature canal and absence of calcifications, anomalies or resorption.
Each tooth will be decoronated using a low-speed diamond saw under copious irrigation to obtain 16±1 mm uniform root lengths.

K-file size #10 / 0.02 taper will be inserted into the canal to check patency. Then the working length will be adjusted using K-file size#15 / 0.02 taper until showing from the apex and then subtracting 1mm to obtain a standard working length of 15 mm.

Each root canal will be prepared using a crown-down technique with the 6 files Dental M3 Pro Gold System 1 . M3 Pro Gold opener size #18 / 0.04 taper will be mounted on a C- Smart 1 pilot and will be inserted into the canal at speed of 300 rpm and torque 1.5 N cm. The second file M3 Pro Gold size #20 / 0.04 taper will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. The third file M3 Pro Gold size #25 / 0.06 taper will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. This will be followed by the fourth file M3 Pro Gold size #30 / 0.04 taper to be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm. The fifth file M3 Pro Gold size #35 / 0.04 taper will be inserted into the canal at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm Followed by file #40 / 0.04 at speed 350 rpm and torque 2 N cm as a final preparatory file.

After each instrument size, the canal will be irrigated using 2 ml 2.5% sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) solution in a 27-gauge slotted, side-vented needle 2 . After complete preparation, the root canal will be rinsed with 2 ml of 17% ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA) solution for 1 min to remove the smear layer. As a final irrigation, 10 ml of distilled water will be done.
Primary preobturation CBCT images will be taken for all teeth collectively before obturation to measure dentin thickness at 3, 6, 9 mm from apex.

Each canal will be dried with absorbent paper points size #40 / 0.04 taper 3 . A radiograph will be obtained to ensure proper GP master cone extension size #40 / 0.04 taper 4 . Each canal will be dried again using paper points.

The samples will be randomly divided according to the type of the root canal sealer and technique of obturation into 4 groups (n=16) Group 1 (CL): Ceraseal in lateral compaction (Control). Group 2 (CV): Ceraseal in vertical compaction. Group 3 (NL): Neosealer flo in lateral compaction. Group 4 (NV): Neosealer flo in vertical compaction.

Group 1 (CL): The Ceraseal sealer will be used to seal the canals utilizing the lateral compaction technique. The sealer will be applied in to the canal using the Intra Canal Tip 5 , followed by the insertion of the master cone #40 / 0.04 taper coated with a thin layer of the sealer. The remaining spaces will be filled using laterally compacted auxiliary gutta-percha cones size #25/ 0.02 6 alongside the main gutta-percha cone. The gutta-percha excess will be then seared off using a heated hand instrument, and access cavities will be sealed using moist cotton pellet.

Group 2 (CV): The Ceraseal sealer will be used in this group to seal the canals utilizing warm vertical compaction technique. A single appropriate plugger 7 will be selected to fit the taper of the canal reaching 3-5 mm short of the canal terminus (Aminsobhani et al.2015). The sealer will be applied in to the canal using the Intra Canal Tip. The master cone will be inserted in to canal to the full working length. The fast pack system unit 8 will be set at a temperature of 200°C and the switch will be placed on touch mode (Aminsobhani et al.2015). The selected plugger will be heat-activated for 2-3 seconds and moved apically in the canal to reach its determined location. Once reached, the heat will be deactivated and a vertical pressure for 10 seconds will be applied by the same plugger. The heat is again activated for 1 second and ceased for 1 second. This activation and deactivation of 1 second each will help the plugger to be removed easily from the canal along with excess GP. The backfill of the canal will be accomplished using a warm gutta- percha injection technique. A fast fill 9 will be set at 180°C and the tip will be placed into the root canal against the apical gutta-percha for 5s before extruding gutta-percha. The mass of gutta-percha will push back the needle coronally and the needle will be removed after a pause of 1s. A plugger will be used to firmly compact the mass of gutta-percha at the orifice level and the access cavity will be sealed using moist cotton pellet.

Group 3 (NL): The Neosealer flo sealer will be used to seal the canals utilizing the modified single-cone technique. The sealer will be applied in to the canal by means of Flo Tip 10 , following the same technique previously mentioned in the CL group and access cavities will be sealed using moist cotton pellet.

Group 4 (NV): The Neosealer flo sealer will be used to seal the canals utilizing the warm vertical compaction technique. The sealer will be applied in to the canal by means of Flo Tip, following the same technique previously mentioned in the CV group and access cavities will be sealed using moist cotton pellet.

To ensure the complete setting of the sealer, all teeth in all groups will be wrapped in a moistened gauze and stored in an incubator at 37°C and 100% humidity for one week in a sealed container (Kharouf et al.2020).

The retreatment procedure will be performed in each tooth. The temporary restoration will be removed, and one drop of D-limonene GP Solvent 11 will be used for 2 min to soften the filling material (Tiyagi et al.2020).
A chronometer will be used to measure net time elapsed from the time of the file entering the canal until it reaches the working length.

Retreatment will be initiated and the gutta-percha in the coronal third will be removed using a # 2 Gates Glidden drill 12 . A 40/.06 instrument (Reciproc) 13 will be used for retreatment procedure. After each movement, the file will be removed from the root canal and cleaned. Canal will be irrigated with approximately 2 mL of 2.5% NaOCl before file reinsertion using 27-gauge slotted, side-vented needle 14 between each instrument change. Then 3 mL of 17% EDTA will be used for 3 min and a final rinse with 2 mL of distilled water will be performed. Removal of the root filling material will be considered finished when there is no more filling debris seen on flutes of the file, working length reached and irrigating solution appears clear.

After the completion of the retreatment procedure, secondary postobturation CBCT imaging will be carried out for all teeth collectively at 3, 6, 9 mm from apex to measure remaining dentin thickness in each sample.
